critic.verdict: An incredibly musical chart with superb vocal sync and comfortable alternating flow, but the density and jump-to-8th transitions push it past standard Easy difficulty limits.

issues.problem: Jumps are followed immediately by 8th notes (0.244s gap). Easy players need at least a quarter or half-note recovery period after jumps to reposition their feet comfortably.
Long 8th-note streams (up to 5 notes) tracking vocal syncopation are too dense for a beginner-level Easy chart, aligning more with Medium/Normal difficulty.
A rapid sequence of very short 1-beat holds (0.488s) is visually confusing and mechanically complex for players at this level.

critic.verdict: A well-structured and highly rhythmic Medium chart that captures the song's energy perfectly, held back only by a few harsh 16th-note spikes.

issues.problem: Landing a JUMP LR followed immediately by a 16th-note tap D at 69.812s is an extremely harsh physical transition for a Medium difficulty chart.
The rapid 16th-note transition from D to U (11.298s to 11.419s) requires a vertical drag or very fast double-step that disrupts flow for intermediate players.
Another vertical 16th-note transition from D to U (95.656s to 95.777s) introduces an unexpected difficulty spike during an otherwise smooth section.

critic.verdict: A decent representation of the song's energy, but marred by several awkward 16th-note streams and jump transitions that force uncomfortable double-steps.

issues.problem: Transitioning from a 16th-note LR jump directly into U and D taps at 16th speed (122ms) is extremely rushed and causes physical balance issues.
The 16th-note transition from R to U forces either an extreme left-foot stretch or a right-foot double-step in just 122ms.
The rapid L -> R (16th) -> D -> U (16th) pattern creates highly unintuitive foot placement, requiring bracket tricks or awkward double-stepping.
The sequence of DL jump -> U -> D -> LR jump featuring 16th notes forces a massive double-step from U to D in 122ms, immediately followed by a jump.
A 16th-note stream of D -> L -> U -> D forces an uncomfortable body spin or a rapid double-step on the Down panel.
